# CodeGraph Benchmark — 2026-07-14

**Branch/HEAD:** `fix/codegraph-freshness-and-memory-audit` @ `cde2c418` · **CLI:** 0.9.5 · generated by `tools/codegraph-benchmark.sh`

## Verdict: ✅ GREEN — Tier-1 all pass, no Tier-2 regression

Tier-1 pass: **14** · Tier-1 fail: **0** · Tier-2 notes: 3 · Tier-2 regressions: **0**

## Follow-up fixes in this PR (2026-07-14)

The tables below are the auto-generated suite output. This PR fixed three defects the
measurement surfaced. **Correction to the earlier report:** the previously-claimed
"`check_citation_compliance` blind spot resolved (11 callers)" was **wrong** — it was
*index pollution*, not an upstream fix (see below). The import-alias blind spot is unchanged.

1. **Nested-worktree index pollution (root cause).** CodeGraph respects `.gitignore`, but
   `.audit-worktrees/` was **not** gitignored, so 10 nested-worktree copies of
   `citation_compliance.py` were indexed. `callers check_citation_compliance` returned **11**,
   but 10 were `.audit-worktrees/*` duplicates and only **1** was the real
   `mira-bots/shared/citation_compliance.py:315` (a genuine *direct* caller,
   `enforce_citation_via_rewrite`). The engine.py **aliased** call (`_check_citation_compliance`)
   still resolves to **0** — so import-alias blind spot #3 stands. **Fix:** gitignore
   `.audit-worktrees/`; the "nested-worktree pollution" Tier-2 row now flags this (10 → will
   clear on next `index --force`). Every `callers`/`callees`/`impact` count was inflated by this.

2. **False-STALE freshness (preflight).** The freshness scan counted *any* newer `.py`/`.ts`/`.tsx`,
   including generated + dependency + nested-worktree paths — a Next.js build or an ad-hoc worktree
   produced a STALE verdict on a current index. Before/after on the live index (`.codegraph` rebuilt
   16:48):

   | | files flagged "newer than index" |
   |---|---|
   | **before** (old scan: only `node_modules`/`.codegraph`/`.git` excluded) | **2763** (all build/worktree noise) |
   | **after** (`cg_newer_indexed_sources`: excludes generated/dep/cache/worktree) | **0** |
   | sanity — after `touch mira-bots/shared/engine.py` | **1** (real edit still detected) |

3. **`.last-sync` marker went stale after a reindex.** Only the git hooks wrote the freshness marker,
   so the daily `codegraph-force-reindex.sh` left it pointing at an old HEAD (preflight then reported
   STALE on a freshly-rebuilt index). **Fix:** the reindex now writes the marker via
   `cg_write_sync_marker`, but only on a confirmed-healthy rebuild.

Fixes live in `tools/codegraph-freshness.sh` (+ `preflight`/`force-reindex` wiring), `.gitignore`,
`.claude/rules/codegraph-usage.md` (blind spot #5), and `tests/test_codegraph_freshness.py` (5 tests).

## Tier-2 — known blind spots (reported; fail only on regression/resolution)
| Case | Expected | Now | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Supervisor` | 0 (blind spot) | 0 | ✅ as-expected (0) — class instantiation not edged (#774) |
| same-name (`resolve_uns_path` ×N defs) | ≥2 defs, can't scope | 10 defs | ✅ as-expected — callers union across defs; grep the file |
| nested-worktree pollution | 0 worktree callers | 10 polluted | ⚠️ index includes nested-worktree copies — gitignore the dir + `index --force` (inflates caller counts) |

> Tier-2 cases are limitations that `grep` covers (see `.claude/rules/codegraph-usage.md` blind spots). A 🎉 RESOLVED row means the limitation was fixed upstream — update the rules + close the relevant issue. A ❌ REGRESSED row means a previously-working relationship broke — treat as a real defect.
